To find the probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A we define the difference variable D as X_B minus X_A. Since both variables are normally distributed the difference D is also normally distributed with mean equal to 75 minus 70 which is 5 and variance equal to 10 squared plus 12 squared which equals 244. The standard deviation of D is the square root of 244 or approximately 15.62. We need the probability that D is greater than 0 so we calculate the z-score as 0 minus 5 divided by 15.62 which gives approximately -0.32. Looking up this z-score in a standard normal table shows the area to the left is about 0.3745 so the area to the right or the probability that D is greater than 0 is approximately 0.6255.

For the performance improvement question we must solve the equation where the exponential function equals the logistic function. The exponential model is 50 times e to the power of 0.02t while the logistic model is 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of negative 0.15 times t minus 5. This equation requires numerical methods because an algebraic solution is not straightforward. The process of finding the exact time t involves iterative approximation or graphing to locate the intersection point. An explanation of the growth curves shows that the exponential curve starts lower but grows steadily while the logistic curve rises sharply around t equals 5 before leveling off. There is nothing simple about solving this transcendental equation by hand so we estimate the intersection occurs near t equals 12.5. No force can make these two distinct mathematical forms equal at multiple points so we accept the single intersection found through approximation.
